description: Compile inclined-plane prompts into deterministic ramp rolling/sliding cases with friction-sensitive downhill motion evidence.
physical_assumptions: The harness coordinate system is z-up.
The ramp downhill axis is +x unless a case explicitly overrides it.
A passive subject starts at rest and should only accelerate downhill under gravity and ramp contact.
Higher dynamic friction should reduce travel distance under the same slope angle and duration.
verifier_rules: Subject trajectory must move downhill along +x.
Subject z should decrease while traveling along the ramp.
Subject must have ramp contact evidence.
Travel distance must fall inside the friction-aware expected range.
repair_suggestions: Ensure the ramp collider is physics-critical and participates in contact events.
Check the downhill axis and coordinate system labels.
Bind dynamic friction to the subject/ramp physics material.
Do not keyframe uphill motion without an explicit force.
